title: Swiggy Menu Power Set
description: Swiggy is testing every possible combo of dishes for a tasting event. Given an array of distinct dish IDs `nums`, return all possible subsets (the power set). Output must be sorted lexicographically on the JSON-stringified version of each subset.

examples.explanation: All 8 subsets, sorted by JSON.stringify lex order (where ',' < ']').

hints: Har element ke liye 2 choice — include or skip.
DFS chala aur path push/pop kar.
Final list ko JSON.stringify se compare karke sort kar.

title: Flipkart Product Tag Combinations
description: Flipkart wants every k-sized combination of product tags from a pool of distinct tag IDs `1..n`. Given integers `n` and `k`, return all combinations (as arrays of integers) sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: DFS me current start index se aage choose kar.
path.length === k pe push and return.
Sort at the end on stringified arrays.

title: Paytm Cashback Distribution Permutations
description: Paytm has a list of distinct cashback amounts to award to N users in some order. Given `nums` of distinct integers, return every permutation. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: Visited array ya boolean[] use kar to avoid reusing same index.
path.length === n pe push.
Final sort on stringified output.

title: Razorpay Subset Hitting Target Volume
description: Razorpay's analytics needs every subset of merchant volumes whose sum equals `target`. Given distinct positive integers `nums` and integer `target`, return all subsets summing to target. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: Subset DFS with running sum; push when sum === target.
Prune jab running sum > target ho jaye.
Lexicographic sort on JSON.stringify at the end.

title: Swiggy Combo Within Budget (Repeat Allowed)
description: Swiggy lets you order the same dish multiple times. Given distinct dish prices `prices` and a target budget `budget`, return every combination (multiset) whose sum equals `budget`. Each inner combination should be in non-decreasing order; the outer list sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: Same index ko reuse karne do — recurse with start = i (not i+1).
Sort the prices first so combinations come out non-decreasing naturally.
Prune when remaining < 0.

title: Zomato Subsets With Duplicate Dishes
description: Zomato has a menu where some dishes are duplicates (same code repeated). Given an array `nums` that may contain duplicates, return the unique power set. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: Sort first so duplicates are adjacent.
Within same DFS level, skip nums[i] when nums[i] === nums[i-1] and i > start.
Final lexicographic sort on JSON.stringify.

title: Ola Driver Rotation With Duplicate Shifts
description: Ola assigns drivers to shifts; some shifts have identical labels. Given `nums` (possibly duplicated), return all unique permutations. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: Sort first.
Used[] array; skip nums[i] if nums[i] === nums[i-1] && !used[i-1] (sibling already used).
Final lexicographic sort.

title: PhonePe OTP Letter Combinations
description: PhonePe is generating mnemonic options for a digit OTP using the classic phone keypad (2->abc, 3->def, 4->ghi, 5->jkl, 6->mno, 7->pqrs, 8->tuv, 9->wxyz). Given a digit string `digits` (each digit in '2'..'9'), return all letter combinations as strings. If `digits` is empty, return `[]`. Output sorted lexicographically (use JSON.stringify ordering, which for strings equals plain lex order).
hints: Map digit to its letter group.
DFS by index; concat letter and recurse.
Sort the final array lexicographically.

title: Myntra Coupon Target Sum (Unique Pick)
description: Myntra has a list of coupon values (may contain duplicates). Each coupon can be used at most once. Return all unique combinations whose sum equals `target`. Each combination in non-decreasing order; outer list sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: Sort to manage duplicates and prune.
Skip nums[i] === nums[i-1] when i > start.
Recurse with start = i + 1 (each used at most once).

title: Flipkart Warehouse Word Search
description: Flipkart's bin-labels grid contains characters. Given a 2D `board` and a `word`, return `true` if the word can be formed by adjacent (up/down/left/right) cells, with each cell used at most once.
hints: DFS from every cell that matches word[0].
Mark visited by overwriting with '#' temporarily; restore after.
Out-of-bounds and char mismatch → return false.

title: Swiggy Balanced Bracket Generator
description: Swiggy's promo engine encodes nested rules with parentheses. Given `n`, return all well-formed parenthesis strings of length `2n`. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: Track open and close counts.
Open '(' allowed if open < n; close ')' allowed if close < open.
Push to result when length === 2n.

title: Amazon Catalog Palindrome Partition
description: Amazon's catalog tagger partitions a product code string `s` into substrings, each of which must be a palindrome. Return every possible palindrome partition. Each partition is a list of strings; outer list sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: DFS over start index; for each end > start, if s[start..end] is palindrome, recurse with start = end + 1.
Helper isPalindrome with two pointers.
Final lexicographic sort.

title: Razorpay Restore IP Addresses
description: Razorpay's webhook log lost the dots in IP addresses. Given a digit string `s`, return all valid IPv4 addresses that can be formed by inserting three dots. A valid octet is 0..255 with no leading zeros ("0" allowed, "01" not allowed). Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: DFS with 4 segments to fill.
Each segment 1..3 chars; reject leading-zero (length>1 and starts with '0') and value > 255.
Stop early if remaining > 3 * remainingSegments or < remainingSegments.

title: Paytm SMS Decode Ways Enumeration
description: Paytm encodes SMS letters as A=1..Z=26. Given a digit string `s`, return all possible decoded strings. If `s` has a leading '0' or any unparsable group, return `[]`. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: DFS by index; take 1 digit if '1'..'9'; take 2 digits if '10'..'26'.
Leading '0' or invalid group → dead end.
Final lexicographic sort.

title: Byju's Decision Tree Paths To Target
description: Byju's adaptive engine models student decisions as a binary tree of integers. Given a tree (as level-order array with `null` for missing children) and `target`, return every root-to-leaf path whose values sum to `target`. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: Build tree from level-order with nulls (BFS using a queue).
DFS down; at each leaf check sum.
Final sort on stringified arrays.

title: Amazon Coupon Code Letter-Case Permutation
description: Amazon coupon codes are case-insensitive — for analytics they want every case variant of a code. Given a string `s` (letters and digits), return all strings reachable by toggling letters' case. Digits stay fixed. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.

examples.explanation: Sorted via localeCompare on JSON-stringified strings; locale order puts lowercase before uppercase.

constraints: s contains letters (a-z, A-Z) and digits
hints: DFS by index; for letter try both lower and upper; for digit only one branch.
Push complete string at i === n.
Final lexicographic sort.

title: Google N-Queens Board Layouts
description: Place `n` queens on an `n x n` chessboard so no two attack each other. Return every solution as a list of strings (one row per string, '.' empty, 'Q' a queen). Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify (i.e. on the array-of-strings).
hints: Place row by row.
Maintain three sets: cols, diag (r - c), anti (r + c).
Sort the final list of boards lexicographically.

title: Microsoft Sudoku Solver
description: Given a 9x9 sudoku `board` (2D array, '.' for empty cells, '1'..'9' otherwise), fill in the empty cells. Return the solved board. Tests use puzzles with a unique solution.

examples.output: the same grid filled per sudoku rules
examples.explanation: Standard sudoku rules: each row/col/3x3 box contains digits 1..9 exactly once.

constraints: Each test has exactly one valid solution
hints: Maintain three sets per row, col, box.
Find the next empty cell, try digits 1..9 that don't conflict, recurse, undo on dead end.
Box index = 3*Math.floor(r/3) + Math.floor(c/3).

title: Uber Rider Route Word Search II
description: Uber's map grid contains characters representing landmarks. Given `board` and a list of `words`, return every word from the list that can be formed on the board by adjacent (up/down/left/right) cells, each cell used once per word. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: For each word run word-search backtracking starting from any matching cell.
Mark visited with sentinel; restore on backtrack.
Push to result if found; final sort lexicographic.

title: Infosys Expression Add Operators
description: Infosys's expression engine inserts '+', '-', or '*' between digits of a numeric string `num` so that the resulting expression evaluates to `target`. Operands cannot have leading zeros (except '0' itself). Return all valid expressions. Output sorted lexicographically on JSON.stringify.
hints: DFS over splits + operator choice.
Track running value and last operand to handle '*' correctly: newValue = val - prev + prev*cur.
Skip operands with leading zero (length > 1 and starts with '0').
